codeReview.summary: The feature mostly implements the requested v1→v2 migration and runtime position reset, but it misses one required safe default and includes a placeholder test that does not verify version stamping behavior.

issues.description: `migrateV1toV2()` does not initialize the required `companions` field for v1 saves. The feature spec explicitly requires missing fields to default to an empty companions collection, but the migration only fills inventory/equipment/stash/quests/exploration/achievements/homestead/settings/difficulty/dialogue/lore-related fields. Loading a true v1 save will still leave `save.companions` absent.

The `save() sets version to CURRENT_SAVE_VERSION (2)` test is only a placeholder: it constructs `new SaveSystem()` and asserts the constant equals 2, but never calls `save()` or verifies the object written to Dexie. This leaves the advertised save-stamping behavior untested despite the feature claiming coverage for it.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered that save-position recovery is enforced at runtime in `ZoneScene.restoreFromSave()` using `campPositions` after map generation, which is an important save/load integration pattern not currently captured in `.factory/library/`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/scenes/ZoneScene.ts:2847-2863 performs the unwalkable-position guard using `findNearestWalkablePosition(...)` and resets the player before continuing restore logic; `.factory/library/architecture.md` does not document this save-position safety pattern.

summary: Reviewed commit `c3cb86b` and the worker transcript/handoff for `save-migration`. The implementation correctly adds versioning, migration, and runtime camp reset logic, but it fails the feature because `companions` is not initialized for migrated v1 saves as required. I also found one non-blocking test-quality gap: the version-stamping test is only a placeholder and does not exercise `SaveSystem.save()`.
